message.content: When I do a vc-x1 sync main bookmark was not updated, but based on our most recent work it should have. Please create a test and where you have a two clones of a repo create a commit and push on repo A then sync repo B and see if the main book mark updates.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: If the Chrome browser tools are deferred (must be loaded via ToolSearch before use), load them with ToolSearch before calling them, and batch every tool you expect to need into ONE ToolSearch call (the select query accepts a comma-separated list). Do NOT load tools one at a time; each separate ToolSearch call wastes a full round-trip.**

Start a browser task whose tools are not yet loaded with a single call loading the core set:

ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp,mcp__claude-in-chrome__navigate,mcp__claude-in-chrome__computer,mcp__claude-in-chrome__read_page,mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_create_mcp"

Add task-specific tools to the same call when the task obviously needs them: read_console_messages / read_network_requests for debugging, form_input for forms, gif_creator for recordings, javascript_tool for page scripting. Only issue a second ToolSearch if the task later needs a tool you did not anticipate.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- claude-in-chrome: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension). - When the user wants to interact with web pages, automate browser tasks, capture screenshots, read console logs, or perform any browser-based actions. Always invoke BEFORE attempting to use any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	//! The `sync` subcommand: fetch + classify + (optionally) rebase /
2	//! fast-forward a workspace's repos against their remotes.
3	//!
4	//! Default is `--check` (fetch + report, error if any repo needs
5	//! action); `--no-check` applies. On any failure every repo is
6	//! reverted to its pre-sync op via `jj op restore`.
7	//!
8	//! `current_op_id` / `op_restore` are `pub(crate)` so `push` reuses
9	//! the same snapshot-and-restore pattern.
10	
11	use std::io::IsTerminal;
12	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
13	use std::process::Command;
14	
15	use clap::Args;
16	use log::{LevelFilter, debug, info, warn};
17	
18	use crate::common::{default_scope, find_workspace_root, prompt, run, scope_to_repos};
19	use crate::context::Context;
20	use crate::desc_helpers::VC_CONFIG_FILE;
21	use crate::options_flags::scope::{Scope, parse_scope};
22	use crate::subcommand::SubcommandRunner;
23	use crate::toml_simple;
24	
25	/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.
26	///
27	/// Default is `--check`: fetch + classify + report, **error** if any
28	/// repo is `behind` or `diverged`. Pass `--no-check` to actually
29	/// rebase / fast-forward. On any failure the starting state of every
30	/// repo is restored via `jj op restore`.
31	///
32	/// Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
33	/// explicitly rather than relying on the default — defaults can shift,
34	/// explicit flags lock in the contract.
35	///
36	/// Repo set is resolved from `-R/--repo` + `--scope`:
37	///
38	/// - `-R PATH` — workspace root, or a single repo to sync alone.
39	/// - `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` — keyword role selection,
40	///   resolved via the workspace root's `.vc-config.toml`.
41	/// - Neither — workspace-default scope:
42	///   - dual workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with `other-repo`) → `code,bot`
43	///   - single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml`, no `other-repo`) → `code`
44	///   - POR (no `.vc-config.toml`) → cwd
45	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
46	pub struct SyncArgs {
47	    /// Verify only — fetch + classify; error if any repo needs action.
48	    /// This is the default; pass explicitly in scripts.
49	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "no_check")]
50	    pub check: bool,
51	
52	    /// Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase / fast-forward as needed.
53	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "check")]
54	    pub no_check: bool,
55	
56	    /// Suppress all informational output (exit code signals result)
57	    #[arg(short, long)]
58	    pub quiet: bool,
59	
60	    /// Bookmark to sync in each repo
61	    #[arg(long, default_value = "main")]
62	    pub bookmark: String,
63	
64	    /// Remote to sync against
65	    #[arg(long, default_value = "origin")]
66	    pub remote: String,
67	
68	    /// Rebase a non-empty `@` onto the synced bookmark without asking.
69	    ///
70	    /// After a successful `--no-check` sync, `@` is repositioned onto
71	    /// the synced bookmark. When the code repo's `@` carries changes
72	    /// it normally asks before rebasing; `--rebase` answers yes up
73	    /// front for non-interactive use. Ignored in `--check` mode and
74	    /// for the session repo (which always `jj new main`).
75	    #[arg(long)]
76	    pub rebase: bool,
77	
78	    /// Workspace root, or a single jj repo to sync on its own.
79	    ///
80	    /// - `-R PATH` alone — sync just the repo at PATH.
81	    /// - `-R PATH -s ROLES` — use PATH as the workspace root and
82	    ///   sync the named side(s).
83	    #[arg(short = 'R', long = "repo", value_name = "PATH", verbatim_doc_comment)]
84	    pub repo: Option<PathBuf>,
85	
86	    /// Which repo(s) of the workspace to sync.
87	    ///
88	    /// `SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot`:
89	    ///
90	    /// - `code` — sync only the app repo.
91	    /// - `bot` — sync only the bot repo (errors if no bot repo
92	    ///   is configured).
93	    /// - `code,bot` — sync both repos.
94	    ///
95	    /// Composes with `-R` as the workspace root. Default depends
96	    /// on workspace state: dual workspace → `code,bot`;
97	    /// single-repo workspace or POR → `code`.
98	    #[arg(
99	        short = 's',
100	        long,
101	        value_name = "SCOPE",
102	        value_parser = parse_scope,
103	        verbatim_doc_comment
104	    )]
105	    pub scope: Option<Scope>,
106	}
107	
108	/// Inputs to the sync op, flat, owned, clap-free.
109	///
110	/// - `quiet`: `-q` / `--quiet` — clamp output to `Warn` for the run.
111	/// - `bookmark`: bookmark to sync in each repo (default `main`).
112	/// - `remote`: remote to sync against (default `origin`).
113	/// - `no_check`: `--no-check` — actually rebase / fast-forward
114	///   (absent ⇒ check mode: fetch + report only). The `--check`
115	///   flag is the explicit form of the default and carries no
116	///   value of its own, so it isn't mirrored here.
117	/// - `rebase`: `--rebase` — rebase a non-empty `@` onto the synced
118	///   bookmark without prompting (code repo only; see
119	///   `reposition_code`).
120	/// - `repo`: `-R/--repo` path (None ⇒ discover the workspace
121	///   root from cwd).
122	/// - `scope`: `--scope` parsed (None ⇒ resolve via the
123	///   workspace-default scope at run time).
124	pub struct SyncParams {
125	    pub quiet: bool,
126	    pub bookmark: String,
127	    pub remote: String,
128	    pub no_check: bool,
129	    pub rebase: bool,
130	    pub repo: Option<PathBuf>,
131	    pub scope: Option<Scope>,
132	}
133	
134	impl From<&SyncArgs> for SyncParams {
135	    /// Convert clap-derived `SyncArgs` into the flat `SyncParams`.
136	    /// `--check` is dropped — it's the explicit form of the
137	    /// default; the op only ever consults `no_check`.
138	    fn from(a: &SyncArgs) -> Self {
139	        Self {
140	            quiet: a.quiet,
141	            bookmark: a.bookmark.clone(),
142	            remote: a.remote.clone(),
143	            no_check: a.no_check,
144	            rebase: a.rebase,
145	            repo: a.repo.clone(),
146	            scope: a.scope.clone(),
147	        }
148	    }
149	}
150	
151	impl SubcommandRunner for SyncArgs {
152	    type Params = SyncParams;
153	
154	    /// Delegate to the existing `From<&SyncArgs>` impl above
155	    /// (total — never fails).
156	    fn to_params(&self) -> Result<Self::Params, String> {
157	        Ok(SyncParams::from(self))
158	    }
159	
160	    /// Run the existing `sync` op.
161	    fn run(ctx: &Context, params: &Self::Params) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
162	        sync(ctx, params)
163	    }
164	}
165	
166	/// Resolve `params` into the concrete repo list `sync_repos`
167	/// operates on.
168	///
169	/// `-R` and `--scope` compose:
170	///
171	/// - neither — workspace-default scope against the discovered root.
172	/// - `-R PATH` alone — sync just that repo.
173	/// - `-s ROLES` alone — roles against the discovered workspace root.
174	/// - `-R PATH -s ROLES` — roles against `PATH` as the workspace root.
175	fn resolve_params_to_repos(
176	    params: &SyncParams,
177	) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
178	    match (&params.repo, &params.scope) {
179	        (None, None) => {
180	            let root = find_workspace_root();
181	            scope_to_repos(&default_scope(root.as_deref()), root.as_deref())
182	        }
183	        (Some(p), None) => Ok(vec![p.clone()]),
184	        (None, Some(s)) => scope_to_repos(s, find_workspace_root().as_deref()),
185	        (Some(p), Some(s)) => scope_to_repos(s, Some(p)),
186	    }
187	}
188	
189	/// Relationship between a local bookmark and its remote counterpart.
190	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
191	pub enum State {
192	    /// Local and remote point at the same commit.
193	    UpToDate,
194	    /// Local is a strict ancestor of remote — fast-forward possible.
195	    Behind { local: String, remote: String },
196	    /// Remote is a strict ancestor of local — nothing to pull in.
197	    Ahead { local: String, remote: String },
198	    /// Neither is an ancestor of the other — needs rebase.
199	    Diverged { local: String, remote: String },
200	    /// The bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart.
201	    NoRemote,
202	}
203	
204	/// Per-repo context accumulated between the snapshot and action phases.
205	#[derive(Debug)]
206	struct RepoCtx {
207	    path: PathBuf,
208	    #[allow(dead_code)]
209	    op_id: String,
210	    state: State,
211	}
212	
213	/// CLI entry point for the `sync` subcommand.
214	///
215	/// Thin wrapper over `sync_repos` that resolves `--scope` into a
216	/// concrete repo list (falling back to the workspace-default
217	/// scope) and forwards the rest. Tests call `sync_repos` directly
218	/// with absolute fixture paths. `ctx` is unused today — present
219	/// for the uniform subcommand-layer signature.
220	///
221	/// When `--quiet` is set, the global log filter is temporarily
222	/// clamped to `Warn` for the duration of the call and restored on
223	/// return, so `info!` calls throughout sync (plus any
224	/// subprocess-stderr routed through `common::run`) go dark. Errors
225	/// still surface at `Warn` / `Error` so script callers don't lose
226	/// diagnostics.
227	pub fn sync(_ctx: &Context, params: &SyncParams) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
228	    let repos = resolve_params_to_repos(params)?;
229	    if params.quiet {
230	        let prev = log::max_level();
231	        log::set_max_level(LevelFilter::Warn);
232	        let result = sync_repos(&repos, params);
233	        log::set_max_level(prev);
234	        result
235	    } else {
236	        sync_repos(&repos, params)
237	    }
238	}
239	
240	/// Sync the given repos against their remotes.
241	///
242	/// Orchestrates the full flow: pre-flight clean-check on every repo,
243	/// snapshot each repo's current op id, then hand off to `run_plan` for
244	/// fetch + classify + (optional) act. On any error, revert every repo
245	/// to its snapshot op via `jj op restore` so the caller sees an atomic
246	/// "either it all went through or nothing changed" outcome.
247	///
248	/// Paths may be relative (resolved against the process cwd) or
249	/// absolute. Tests use absolute tempdir paths to avoid cwd dependence
250	/// under parallel `cargo test`.
251	pub fn sync_repos(
252	    repos: &[PathBuf],
253	    params: &SyncParams,
254	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
255	    debug!(
256	        "sync: enter (no_check={}, bookmark={}, remote={})",
257	        params.no_check, params.bookmark, params.remote
258	    );
259	
260	    // Preflight: verify bookmark tracking on every repo before any
261	    // fetch/rebase. Implements "Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection"
262	    // — design at:
263	    //   https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/blob/main/notes/chores/chores-06.md#non-tracking-remote-bookmark-detection-design
264	    for repo in repos {
265	        crate::common::verify_tracking(repo, &params.bookmark)?;
266	    }
267	
268	    let mut snapshots: Vec<(PathBuf, String)> = Vec::new();
269	    for repo in repos {
270	        let op_id = current_op_id(repo)?;
271	        debug!("{}: op snapshot = {op_id}", repo.display());
272	        snapshots.push((repo.clone(), op_id));
273	    }
274	
275	    let result = run_plan(&snapshots, params);
276	
277	    if let Err(e) = &result {
278	        warn!("sync failed: {e}");
279	        warn!("reverting all repos to starting state...");
280	        for (repo, op_id) in &snapshots {
281	            match op_restore(repo, op_id) {
282	                Ok(()) => info!("  {}: reverted to op {op_id}", repo.display()),
283	                Err(re) => warn!("  {}: revert failed: {re}", repo.display()),
284	            }
285	        }
286	        debug!("sync: exit");
287	        return result;
288	    }
289	
290	    // Reposition `@` onto the freshly-synced bookmark. Runs only in
291	    // apply mode and only after every repo synced cleanly, and sits
292	    // OUTSIDE the `op_restore` revert region above: a reposition
293	    // failure (e.g. the session repo's `@-` off main) is surfaced
294	    // without rolling back the successful fetch / fast-forward.
295	    if params.no_check {
296	        for (repo, _) in &snapshots {
297	            reposition_at(repo, &params.bookmark, params)?;
298	        }
299	    }
300	
301	    debug!("sync: exit");
302	    Ok(())
303	}
304	
305	/// Fetch and classify each repo, then act (or not, in dry-run).
306	///
307	/// Returns `Err` on the first failure so the caller can trigger the
308	/// cross-repo revert; partial success across repos is *not* a valid
309	/// end state for this command.
310	fn run_plan(
311	    snapshots: &[(PathBuf, String)],
312	    params: &SyncParams,
313	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
314	    // Phase 1 — fetch + classify silently.
315	    //
316	    // Each repo's fetch stderr is captured (rather than streamed to
317	    // `info!` via `common::run`) so we can decide after classification
318	    // whether to surface it. `jj git fetch`'s routine "Nothing
319	    // changed." chatter is the main thing we're suppressing here —
320	    // if nothing needs action, the user shouldn't see it.
321	    let mut fetched: Vec<(PathBuf, String)> = Vec::new();
322	    let mut ctxs: Vec<RepoCtx> = Vec::new();
323	    for (repo, op_id) in snapshots {
324	        let stderr = fetch_silent(repo, &params.remote)?;
325	        fetched.push((repo.clone(), stderr));
326	        let state = classify(repo, &params.bookmark, &params.remote)?;
327	        ctxs.push(RepoCtx {
328	            path: repo.clone(),
329	            op_id: op_id.clone(),
330	            state,
331	        });
332	    }
333	
334	    let any_action_needed = ctxs
335	        .iter()
336	        .any(|c| matches!(c.state, State::Behind { .. } | State::Diverged { .. }));
337	
338	    // Phase 2 — emit status. `--quiet` is enforced globally via the
339	    // log-level clamp in `sync()`, so these `info!` calls are already
340	    // suppressed in scripts; we just shape the output here.
341	    if !any_action_needed {
342	        let n = ctxs.len();
343	        let noun = if n == 1 { "repo" } else { "repos" };
344	        info!("sync: {n} {noun}, all bookmarks up-to-date");
345	    } else {
346	        for (repo, stderr) in &fetched {
347	            info!("{}: fetch {}", repo.display(), params.remote);
348	            for line in stderr.lines() {
349	                info!("{line}");
350	            }
351	        }
352	        for ctx in &ctxs {
353	            log_state(&ctx.path, &ctx.state);
354	        }
355	    }
356	
357	    // Phase 3 — act (subprocess output streams through as usual).
358	    // `act_on_state` short-circuits when `params.no_check` is false,
359	    // so check mode is a true no-op here. Repositioning `@` onto the
360	    // synced bookmark happens after `run_plan` returns (see
361	    // `sync_repos`), outside the revert region.
362	    for ctx in &ctxs {
363	        act_on_state(ctx, params)?;
364	    }
365	
366	    // Phase 4 — check mode is fatal when action would be needed.
367	    // Apply mode (`--no-check`) ran the action above and is done.
368	    if !params.no_check && any_action_needed {
369	        let n_action = ctxs
370	            .iter()
371	            .filter(|c| matches!(c.state, State::Behind { .. } | State::Diverged { .. }))
372	            .count();
373	        let noun = if n_action == 1 { "repo" } else { "repos" };
374	        return Err(format!(
375	            "sync: {n_action} {noun} need action (see above) — \
376	             resolve with `vc-x1 sync --no-check` and re-run"
377	        )
378	        .into());
379	    }
380	    Ok(())
381	}
382	
383	/// Fetch `repo` from `remote` without streaming subprocess output to
384	/// `info!`.
385	///
386	/// Mirrors what `common::run` would do, but returns stderr to the
387	/// caller so the caller can decide whether to surface it (verbose /
388	/// action case) or drop it (clean case). Stdout is dropped — `jj git
389	/// fetch` doesn't use it. Failure carries stderr in the error message.
390	fn fetch_silent(repo: &Path, remote: &str) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
391	    debug!("$ jj git fetch --remote {remote} -R {}", repo.display());
392	    let output = Command::new("jj")
393	        .args(["git", "fetch", "--remote", remote, "-R", &repo_str(repo)])
394	        .output()
395	        .map_err(|e| format!("failed to run jj git fetch: {e}"))?;
396	    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).trim().to_string();
397	    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).trim().to_string();
398	    if !stdout.is_empty() {
399	        debug!("  {stdout}");
400	    }
401	    if !output.status.success() {
402	        return Err(format!("jj git fetch -R {} failed: {stderr}", repo.display()).into());
403	    }
404	    Ok(stderr)
405	}
406	
407	/// Reposition `@` onto the freshly-synced bookmark after a successful
408	/// apply-mode sync.
409	///
410	/// Dispatches on repo role — the caller has already gated on apply
411	/// mode:
412	///
413	/// - **session (bot) sub-repo** → always `jj new main`
414	///   (see `reposition_session`).
415	/// - **any other repo** → move `@` onto the synced `bookmark` under
416	///   the code-repo safety rules (see `reposition_code`).
417	fn reposition_at(
418	    repo: &Path,
419	    bookmark: &str,
420	    params: &SyncParams,
421	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
422	    if is_session_repo(repo) {
423	        reposition_session(repo)
424	    } else {
425	        reposition_code(repo, bookmark, params.rebase)
426	    }
427	}
428	
429	/// True when `repo` is the session (bot) sub-repo.
430	///
431	/// Reads `<repo>/.vc-config.toml`'s `[workspace] path`: the code repo
432	/// (workspace root) is `"/"`, the session sub-repo is `"/.claude"`. A
433	/// missing / unreadable config (POR, single-repo workspace) is treated
434	/// as a code repo.
435	fn is_session_repo(repo: &Path) -> bool {
436	    match toml_simple::toml_load(&repo.join(VC_CONFIG_FILE)) {
437	        Ok(cfg) => {
438	            toml_simple::toml_get(&cfg, "workspace.path").map(String::as_str) == Some("/.claude")
439	        }
440	        Err(_) => false,
441	    }
442	}
443	
444	/// Reposition the session repo's `@` onto `main`.
445	///
446	/// The session (`.claude`) repo is a linear journal on `main`, and its
447	/// `@` normally carries live session writes:
448	///
449	/// - Errors when `@-` isn't on `main` (not an ancestor-or-equal of the
450	///   bookmark) — refuse rather than guess.
451	/// - Otherwise `jj new main` starts a fresh `@` on the bookmark; the
452	///   prior `@` becomes a sibling head, which is expected for the
453	///   journal. A conflict is very unlikely given `.claude`'s content;
454	///   if one ever appears the user resolves it.
455	fn reposition_session(repo: &Path) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
456	    let parent = commit_id(repo, "@-")?;
457	    if !revset_nonempty(repo, &format!("{parent}::main"))? {
458	        return Err(format!(
459	            "{}: @- ({parent}) is not on main — refusing to reposition @",
460	            repo.display()
461	        )
462	        .into());
463	    }
464	    info!("{}: jj new main", repo.display());
465	    run(
466	        "jj",
467	        &["new", "main", "-R", &repo_str(repo)],
468	        Path::new("."),
469	    )?;
470	    Ok(())
471	}
472	
473	/// Reposition the code repo's `@` onto the synced `bookmark`.
474	///
475	/// Let `@-` be the parent of `@`:
476	///
477	/// - `bookmark == @-` → already positioned; no-op.
478	/// - `bookmark` a proper descendant of `@-`, `@` empty →
479	///   `jj new bookmark` (jj auto-abandons the old empty `@`).
480	/// - `bookmark` a proper descendant of `@-`, `@` non-empty → rebase
481	///   `@` onto `bookmark`, but only with `rebase` set (else prompt on a
482	///   TTY; skip + inform when declined or not a TTY).
483	/// - `bookmark` not a descendant of `@-` (diverged / `@` ahead) →
484	///   leave `@` and inform why it didn't move.
485	fn reposition_code(
486	    repo: &Path,
487	    bookmark: &str,
488	    rebase: bool,
489	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
490	    let parent = commit_id(repo, "@-")?;
491	    let tip = commit_id(repo, bookmark)?;
492	    if tip == parent {
493	        info!("{}: @ already on '{bookmark}'", repo.display());
494	        return Ok(());
495	    }
496	    if !revset_nonempty(repo, &format!("{parent}::{tip}"))? {
497	        info!(
498	            "{}: @- ({parent}) is not behind '{bookmark}' ({tip}); leaving @ in place",
499	            repo.display()
500	        );
501	        return Ok(());
502	    }
503	    // `bookmark` is a proper descendant of `@-` — safe to move a clean `@`.
504	    if at_is_empty(repo)? {
505	        info!("{}: jj new {bookmark}", repo.display());
506	        run(
507	            "jj",
508	            &["new", bookmark, "-R", &repo_str(repo)],
509	            Path::new("."),
510	        )?;
511	        return Ok(());
512	    }
513	    // `@` carries changes: rebase only on opt-in / confirmation.
514	    if !rebase && !confirm_rebase(repo)? {
515	        info!(
516	            "{}: @ has changes; left in place (pass --rebase to rebase onto '{bookmark}')",
517	            repo.display()
518	        );
519	        return Ok(());
520	    }
521	    info!("{}: rebasing @ onto '{bookmark}'", repo.display());
522	    run(
523	        "jj",
524	        &["rebase", "-b", "@", "-d", bookmark, "-R", &repo_str(repo)],
525	        Path::new("."),
526	    )?;
527	    if has_conflicts(repo)? {
528	        return Err(format!(
529	            "{}: rebase of @ onto '{bookmark}' produced conflicts",
530	            repo.display()
531	        )
532	        .into());
533	    }
534	    Ok(())
535	}
536	
537	/// Ask whether to rebase a non-empty `@`, but only on a TTY.
538	///
539	/// Returns `Ok(false)` without prompting when stdin isn't a terminal
540	/// (scripts): the caller then skips + informs rather than blocking on
541	/// `read_line`. A `y`/`yes` (case-insensitive) answer confirms.
542	fn confirm_rebase(repo: &Path) -> Result<bool, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
543	    if !std::io::stdin().is_terminal() {
544	        return Ok(false);
545	    }
546	    let ans = prompt(&format!(
547	        "{}: @ has changes — rebase onto the synced bookmark? [y/N] ",
548	        repo.display()
549	    ))?;
550	    Ok(matches!(ans.to_ascii_lowercase().as_str(), "y" | "yes"))
551	}
552	
553	/// True when the working-copy commit `@` is empty (no changes).
554	fn at_is_empty(repo: &Path) -> Result<bool, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
555	    revset_nonempty(repo, "@ & empty()")
556	}
557	
558	/// Perform the mutation corresponding to `ctx.state` when `--no-check`.
559	///
560	/// - `UpToDate` / `Ahead` / `NoRemote` → no-op (and no output, the state
561	///   was already logged by `log_state`).
562	/// - `Behind` → `jj bookmark set <b> -r <b>@<remote>` to fast-forward.
563	/// - `Diverged` → `jj rebase -b <b> -d <b>@<remote>`, then probe
564	///   `conflicts()`. A non-empty result means the rebase produced
565	///   conflicted commits; return `Err` so the outer revert restores the
566	///   pre-fetch state.
567	fn act_on_state(ctx: &RepoCtx, params: &SyncParams) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
568	    let repo = &ctx.path;
569	    let remote_rev = format!("{}@{}", params.bookmark, params.remote);
570	    match &ctx.state {
571	        State::UpToDate | State::Ahead { .. } | State::NoRemote => Ok(()),
572	        State::Behind { .. } => {
573	            if params.no_check {
574	                info!("{}: fast-forwarding '{}'", repo.display(), params.bookmark);
575	                run(
576	                    "jj",
577	                    &[
578	                        "bookmark",
579	                        "set",
580	                        &params.bookmark,
581	                        "-r",
582	                        &remote_rev,
583	                        "-R",
584	                        &repo_str(repo),
585	                    ],
586	                    Path::new("."),
587	                )?;
588	            }
589	            Ok(())
590	        }
591	        State::Diverged { local, remote } => {
592	            if params.no_check {
593	                // `local` is either a single commit id or a comma-joined list
594	                // of heads when the bookmark is conflicted. Pick the head
595	                // that isn't the remote — that's the local-only tip. The
596	                // comma-joined path covers the jj post-fetch divergence
597	                // shape (local bookmark conflicted between old local head
598	                // and freshly-fetched remote head).
599	                let local_head = local
600	                    .split(',')
601	                    .find(|h| *h != remote)
602	                    .unwrap_or(local.as_str());
603	                info!(
604	                    "{}: rebasing {local_head} onto {remote_rev}",
605	                    repo.display()
606	                );
607	                run(
608	                    "jj",
609	                    &[
610	                        "rebase",
611	                        "-b",
612	                        local_head,
613	                        "-d",
614	                        &remote_rev,
615	                        "-R",
616	                        &repo_str(repo),
617	                    ],
618	                    Path::new("."),
619	                )?;
620	                if has_conflicts(repo)? {
621	                    return Err(format!("{}: rebase produced conflicts", repo.display()).into());
622	                }
623	            }
624	            Ok(())
625	        }
626	    }
627	}
628	
629	/// Print a single-line summary of `state` at `info!` level.
630	fn log_state(repo: &Path, state: &State) {
631	    let r = repo.display();
632	    match state {
633	        State::UpToDate => info!("{r}: up-to-date"),
634	        State::NoRemote => info!("{r}: no remote counterpart — skipping"),
635	        State::Ahead { local, remote } => {
636	            info!("{r}: ahead (local {local} > remote {remote}); nothing to sync")
637	        }
638	        State::Behind { local, remote } => {
639	            info!("{r}: behind (local {local} < remote {remote}); would fast-forward")
640	        }
641	        State::Diverged { local, remote } => {
642	            info!("{r}: diverged (local {local} vs remote {remote}); would rebase")
643	        }
644	    }
645	}
646	
647	/// Return the id of the most recent operation on `repo`.
648	///
649	/// Used as the revert target on failure. Exposed at `pub(crate)` so
650	/// `push` (0.37.0-2+) can reuse the same snapshot pattern for its
651	/// commit-stage rollback without duplicating the jj invocation.
652	pub(crate) fn current_op_id(repo: &Path) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
653	    let out = run(
654	        "jj",
655	        &[
656	            "op",
657	            "log",
658	            "--no-graph",
659	            "-n",
660	            "1",
661	            "-T",
662	            "id.short(12)",
663	            "-R",
664	            &repo_str(repo),
665	        ],
666	        Path::new("."),
667	    )?;
668	    Ok(out.trim().to_string())
669	}
670	
671	/// Restore `repo` to the operation identified by `op_id`.
672	///
673	/// Thin wrapper around `jj op restore`. Called during the failure
674	/// revert path — drops the caller's returned stdout. Exposed at
675	/// `pub(crate)` so `push` reuses the same restore call.
676	pub(crate) fn op_restore(repo: &Path, op_id: &str) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
677	    run(
678	        "jj",
679	        &["op", "restore", op_id, "-R", &repo_str(repo)],
680	        Path::new("."),
681	    )?;
682	    Ok(())
683	}
684	
685	/// Classify the relationship between `bookmark` and `bookmark@remote`.
686	///
687	/// Uses `bookmarks(<b>)` rather than the bare name so a conflicted
688	/// bookmark (jj's representation of a diverged fetch) resolves to all
689	/// of its heads instead of erroring. When the set has multiple heads,
690	/// the bookmark is conflicted and the repo is `Diverged` by definition.
691	/// Otherwise we compare the single local head against the single
692	/// remote commit via two revset-ancestry probes.
693	///
694	/// Returns `NoRemote` when `<b>@<remote>` does not resolve — the caller
695	/// logs a skip and moves on.
696	fn classify(
697	    repo: &Path,
698	    bookmark: &str,
699	    remote: &str,
700	) -> Result<State, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
701	    let local_heads = local_bookmark_heads(repo, bookmark)?;
702	    let remote_rev = format!("{bookmark}@{remote}");
703	    let Some(remote) = try_commit_id(repo, &remote_rev)? else {
704	        return Ok(State::NoRemote);
705	    };
706	    if local_heads.is_empty() {
707	        return Err(format!("{}: bookmark '{bookmark}' does not exist", repo.display()).into());
708	    }
709	    if local_heads.len() > 1 {
710	        // Conflicted bookmark — jj's shape for post-fetch divergence.
711	        let local = local_heads.join(",");
712	        return Ok(State::Diverged { local, remote });
713	    }
714	    let local = local_heads.into_iter().next().unwrap(); // OK: `len() > 1` arm handled above, `is_empty()` handled above
715	    if local == remote {
716	        return Ok(State::UpToDate);
717	    }
718	    let local_is_anc = revset_nonempty(repo, &format!("{local}::{remote_rev}"))?;
719	    let remote_is_anc = revset_nonempty(repo, &format!("{remote_rev}::{local}"))?;
720	    Ok(match (local_is_anc, remote_is_anc) {
721	        (true, _) => State::Behind { local, remote },
722	        (false, true) => State::Ahead { local, remote },
723	        (false, false) => State::Diverged { local, remote },
724	    })
725	}
726	
727	/// Return all commit ids the `bookmark` currently points at.
728	///
729	/// Normally a one-element vector; two or more elements indicate a
730	/// conflicted bookmark (jj's representation of diverged post-fetch
731	/// state, where the local bookmark has multiple heads).
732	fn local_bookmark_heads(
733	    repo: &Path,
734	    bookmark: &str,
735	) -> Result<Vec<String>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
736	    let out = run(
737	        "jj",
738	        &[
739	            "log",
740	            "-r",
741	            &format!("bookmarks(exact:{bookmark})"),
742	            "--no-graph",
743	            "-T",
744	            r#"commit_id.short(12) ++ "\n""#,
745	            "-R",
746	            &repo_str(repo),
747	        ],
748	        Path::new("."),
749	    )?;
750	    Ok(out
751	        .lines()
752	        .map(|l| l.trim().to_string())
753	        .filter(|l| !l.is_empty())
754	        .collect())
755	}
756	
757	/// Return the short commit id that `rev` resolves to in `repo`.
758	///
759	/// Errors when the revset is unresolvable or matches multiple heads
760	/// — callers that want to tolerate "revset doesn't resolve" should use
761	/// `try_commit_id`.
762	fn commit_id(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
763	    let out = run(
764	        "jj",
765	        &[
766	            "log",
767	            "-r",
768	            rev,
769	            "--no-graph",
770	            "-T",
771	            "commit_id.short(12)",
772	            "-R",
773	            &repo_str(repo),
774	        ],
775	        Path::new("."),
776	    )?;
777	    Ok(out.trim().to_string())
778	}
779	
780	/// Like `commit_id`, but `Ok(None)` when the revset doesn't resolve.
781	///
782	/// jj reports missing revisions via stderr strings like
783	/// `Revision \`foo@origin\` doesn't exist` or `No such revision …`;
784	/// both get mapped to `Ok(None)` so callers can distinguish "missing"
785	/// from "other subprocess failure".
786	fn try_commit_id(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> Result<Option<String>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
787	    match commit_id(repo, rev) {
788	        Ok(id) if id.is_empty() => Ok(None),
789	        Ok(id) => Ok(Some(id)),
790	        Err(e) => {
791	            let msg = e.to_string();
792	            if msg.contains("doesn't exist") || msg.contains("No such revision") {
793	                Ok(None)
794	            } else {
795	                Err(e)
796	            }
797	        }
798	    }
799	}
800	
801	/// Return `true` when `revset` matches at least one commit in `repo`.
802	fn revset_nonempty(repo: &Path, revset: &str) -> Result<bool, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
803	    let out = run(
804	        "jj",
805	        &[
806	            "log",
807	            "-r",
808	            revset,
809	            "--no-graph",
810	            "-T",
811	            r#"commit_id.short() ++ "\n""#,
812	            "-R",
813	            &repo_str(repo),
814	        ],
815	        Path::new("."),
816	    )?;
817	    Ok(!out.trim().is_empty())
818	}
819	
820	/// Return `true` when `repo` has any conflicted commits.
821	fn has_conflicts(repo: &Path) -> Result<bool, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
822	    revset_nonempty(repo, "conflicts()")
823	}
824	
825	/// Convert a path to a `String` suitable for passing as a subprocess arg.
826	fn repo_str(p: &Path) -> String {
827	    p.to_string_lossy().into_owned()
828	}
829	
830	#[cfg(test)]
831	mod tests;
832	
833	#[cfg(test)]
834	mod integration_tests;
835

lastPrompt: When I do a vc-x1 sync main bookmark was not updated, but based on our most recent work it should have. Please create a test and where you have a two clones of a repo create a commit and push on repo…

aiTitle: Test main bookmark update on vc-x1 sync

content.text: Now let me look at the existing integration tests to see the fixture patterns for two clones.

content.content:
1	//! Integration tests for the sync module.
2	//!
3	//! End-to-end tests for `sync_repos` against real dual-repo jj
4	//! fixtures. Each test builds an isolated fixture (bare-git
5	//! remotes + colocated jj repos) under a unique tempdir via
6	//! `Fixture::new`, then drives the scenario with plain `jj`
7	//! subprocess calls. Requires `jj` in `PATH`.
8	//!
9	//! Fixtures clean themselves up via `Fixture`'s `Drop` impl so a
10	//! panicking test still removes its tempdir.
11	
12	use super::*;
13	use crate::options_flags::scope::Side;
14	use crate::test_helpers::Fixture;
15	use std::fs;
16	use std::process::Command;
17	
18	use crate::common::{default_scope, find_workspace_root_from, scope_to_repos};
19	
20	/// Resolver helpers must consume what `init --repo-local` produces.
21	///
22	/// Builds a real dual-repo fixture (bare git remotes + colocated jj
23	/// repos + the canonical `.vc-config.toml` pair init writes), then
24	/// drives the resolver chain `sync()` uses when `-R` is empty:
25	///
26	/// - `find_workspace_root_from(&fx.claude)` walks up to `fx.work`
27	///   (proves cwd-portability against init's `path = "/.claude"` /
28	///   `path = "/"` config split).
29	/// - `default_scope(Some(&fx.work))` reads the workspace config and
30	///   resolves to the dual-repo default.
31	/// - `scope_to_repos` maps each `Scope` shape to the right
32	///   absolute path(s) under the fixture.
33	///
34	/// Pure check on the resolver chain — does not invoke `sync()`
35	/// itself, since that walks `std::env::current_dir()` and parallel
36	/// `cargo test` makes cwd mutation unsafe.
37	#[test]
38	fn resolver_chain_against_init_repo_local() {
39	    let fx = Fixture::new("resolver-chain");
40	
41	    // Walk-up from the bot side lands on the app root.
42	    assert_eq!(
43	        find_workspace_root_from(&fx.claude).as_deref(),
44	        Some(&*fx.work),
45	        "find_workspace_root should resolve from .claude up to work"
46	    );
47	    // Walk-up from the app root finds itself.
48	    assert_eq!(
49	        find_workspace_root_from(&fx.work).as_deref(),
50	        Some(&*fx.work)
51	    );
52	
53	    // init --repo-local writes other-repo = ".claude", so the
54	    // workspace's default scope is dual.
55	    assert_eq!(
56	        default_scope(Some(&fx.work)),
57	        Scope(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot])
58	    );
59	
60	    // Each scope shape resolves to the right absolute path(s).
61	    assert_eq!(
62	        scope_to_repos(&Scope(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap(),
63	        vec![fx.work.clone(), fx.claude.clone()]
64	    );
65	    assert_eq!(
66	        scope_to_repos(&Scope(vec![Side::Code]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap(),
67	        vec![fx.work.clone()]
68	    );
69	    assert_eq!(
70	        scope_to_repos(&Scope(vec![Side::Bot]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap(),
71	        vec![fx.claude.clone()]
72	    );
73	
74	    // sync_repos accepts the resolved list and reports up-to-date
75	    // — the resolver's output is shaped the way sync expects.
76	    let resolved = scope_to_repos(&Scope(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap();
77	    sync_repos(&resolved, &apply_params()).expect("sync should succeed on resolved repos");
78	}
79	
80	/// Run `jj <args> -R <repo>` and assert success; returns trimmed stdout.
81	fn jj(repo: &Path, args: &[&str]) -> String {
82	    let out = Command::new("jj")
83	        .args(args)
84	        .arg("-R")
85	        .arg(repo)
86	        .output()
87	        .expect("spawn jj");
88	    assert!(
89	        out.status.success(),
90	        "jj {args:?} failed in {}: {}",
91	        repo.display(),
92	        String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr)
93	    );
94	    String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stdout).trim().to_string()
95	}
96	
97	/// Resolve `rev` to its short commit id in `repo`.
98	fn cid(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
99	    jj(
100	        repo,
101	        &["log", "-r", rev, "--no-graph", "-T", "commit_id.short(12)"],
102	    )
103	}
104	
105	/// Sync params with `--no-check` set (apply mode).
106	///
107	/// Integration tests pass explicit repo paths through `sync_repos`
108	/// directly, so `repo` / `scope` stay `None` here and the CLI-side
109	/// default resolution is not exercised by this helper.
110	fn apply_params() -> SyncParams {
111	    SyncParams {
112	        no_check: true,
113	        quiet: false,
114	        bookmark: "main".to_string(),
115	        remote: "origin".to_string(),
116	        rebase: false,
117	        repo: None,
118	        scope: None,
119	    }
120	}
121	
122	/// Apply-mode params with `--rebase` set (auto-confirm the code-repo
123	/// non-empty `@` rebase).
124	fn rebase_params() -> SyncParams {
125	    SyncParams {
126	        rebase: true,
127	        ..apply_params()
128	    }
129	}
130	
131	/// True when `revset` matches at least one commit in `repo`.
132	fn has(repo: &Path, revset: &str) -> bool {
133	    !jj(
134	        repo,
135	        &[
136	            "log",
137	            "-r",
138	            revset,
139	            "--no-graph",
140	            "-T",
141	            r#"commit_id.short(12) ++ "\n""#,
142	        ],
143	    )
144	    .trim()
145	    .is_empty()
146	}
147	
148	/// Add a local-only commit on `main` in `repo` (not pushed), then
149	/// restore `@` to an empty child so pre-flight still passes.
150	///
151	/// Sequence: write file → describe `@` → advance `main` to `@` →
152	/// create a fresh empty `@` above it.
153	fn add_local_commit(repo: &Path, file: &str, content: &str, msg: &str) {
154	    fs::write(repo.join(file), content).expect("write local file");
155	    jj(repo, &["describe", "@", "-m", msg]);
156	    jj(repo, &["bookmark", "set", "main", "-r", "@"]);
157	    jj(repo, &["new"]);
158	}
159	
160	/// Clone `remote_url` into `<base>/<work_name>`, add a commit, push it.
161	///
162	/// Used to make the remote advance beyond the fixture's `main`
163	/// from a separate working copy. Returns the pushed commit's id.
164	fn push_from_clone(
165	    base: &Path,
166	    remote_url: &Path,
167	    work_name: &str,
168	    file: &str,
169	    content: &str,
170	    msg: &str,
171	) -> String {
172	    let workdir = base.join(work_name);
173	    let out = Command::new("jj")
174	        .args(["git", "clone", "--colocate"])
175	        .arg(remote_url)
176	        .arg(&workdir)
177	        .output()
178	        .expect("spawn jj clone");
179	    assert!(
180	        out.status.success(),
181	        "jj git clone failed: {}",
182	        String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr)
183	    );
184	    fs::write(workdir.join(file), content).expect("write remote file");
185	    jj(&workdir, &["describe", "@", "-m", msg]);
186	    jj(&workdir, &["bookmark", "set", "main", "-r", "@"]);
187	    jj(&workdir, &["git", "push", "--bookmark", "main"]);
188	    cid(&workdir, "main")
189	}
190	
191	/// Scenario 1: fresh fixture, nothing to do — `sync` leaves both
192	/// repos untouched.
193	#[test]
194	fn sync_up_to_date() {
195	    let fx = Fixture::new("up-to-date");
196	    let work_main = cid(&fx.work, "main");
197	    let claude_main = cid(&fx.claude, "main");
198	    sync_repos(&fx.repos(), &apply_params()).expect("sync should succeed");
199	    assert_eq!(cid(&fx.work, "main"), work_main);
200	    assert_eq!(cid(&fx.claude, "main"), claude_main);
201	}
202	
203	/// Scenario 2a: a non-empty `@` on top of main (simulates `/exit`
204	/// trailing session writes in `.claude`) when there's nothing new on
205	/// the remote. The session repo always `jj new main`s: `@` becomes a
206	/// fresh empty child of the unmoved main, and the trailing commit is
207	/// preserved as a non-empty sibling head (no longer in the working
208	/// copy).
209	#[test]
210	fn sync_session_jj_new_when_up_to_date() {
211	    let fx = Fixture::new("session-jjnew-uptodate");
212	    let pre_main = cid(&fx.claude, "main");
213	    fs::write(fx.claude.join("trailing.jsonl"), "{\"line\":1}\n").expect("write trailing file");
214	    sync_repos(&fx.repos(), &apply_params()).expect("sync should succeed");
215	    // main didn't move.
216	    assert_eq!(cid(&fx.claude, "main"), pre_main, "main should not move");
217	    // @ is a fresh empty child of main.
218	    assert!(has(&fx.claude, "@ & empty()"), "@ should be empty");
219	    assert!(has(&fx.claude, "main::@"), "@ should be a child of main");
220	    // The trailing session commit survives as a non-empty sibling head.
221	    assert!(
222	        has(&fx.claude, "heads(all()) & ~empty()"),
223	        "former @ preserved as a non-empty sibling head"
224	    );
225	    // The trailing file is no longer in the working copy (@ moved off it).
226	    assert!(
227	        !fx.claude.join("trailing.jsonl").exists(),
228	        "@ no longer holds the trailing file"
229	    );
230	}
231	
232	/// Scenario 2b: `@` has trailing writes and the remote advanced while
233	/// the session was offline. jj's fetch auto-ff's main; the session
234	/// repo then `jj new main`s onto the new tip, leaving the trailing
235	/// commit as a sibling head off the old tip.
236	#[test]
237	fn sync_session_jj_new_when_main_moves() {
238	    let fx = Fixture::new("session-jjnew-moved");
239	    let remote_claude = fx.base.join("remote-claude.git");
240	    let remote_head = push_from_clone(
241	        &fx.base,
242	        &remote_claude,
243	        "claude2",
244	        "remote.md",
245	        "remote\n",
246	        "feat: remote-added",
247	    );
248	    // Trailing writes on @
249	    fs::write(fx.claude.join("trailing.jsonl"), "{\"line\":2}\n").expect("write trailing file");
250	    sync_repos(&fx.repos(), &apply_params()).expect("sync should succeed");
251	    assert_eq!(
252	        cid(&fx.claude, "main"),
253	        remote_head,
254	        "local main should match remote after auto-ff"
255	    );
256	    // @ is a fresh empty child of the new main.
257	    assert!(has(&fx.claude, "@ & empty()"), "@ should be empty");
258	    assert!(has(&fx.claude, "main::@"), "@ should be a child of main");
259	    // The trailing session commit survives as a non-empty sibling head.
260	    assert!(
261	        has(&fx.claude, "heads(all()) & ~empty()"),
262	        "former @ preserved as a non-empty sibling head"
263	    );
264	    // The trailing file is no longer in the working copy.
265	    assert!(
266	        !fx.claude.join("trailing.jsonl").exists(),
267	        "@ no longer holds the trailing file"
268	    );
269	}
270	
271	/// Scenario 2c: the session repo refuses to reposition when `@-` is
272	/// not on main. A local session commit ahead of main (main left
273	/// behind) puts `@-` off main's line, so `jj new main` would strand
274	/// it — sync errors instead.
275	#[test]
276	fn sync_session_errors_when_at_parent_off_main() {
277	    let fx = Fixture::new("session-off-main");
278	    // A described commit ahead of main, with a fresh @ above it, so
279	    // @- is ahead of (not on) main.
280	    fs::write(fx.claude.join("ahead.jsonl"), "{\"line\":9}\n").expect("write ahead file");
281	    jj(&fx.claude, &["describe", "@", "-m", "feat: session ahead"]);
282	    jj(&fx.claude, &["new"]);
283	
284	    let err = sync_repos(&fx.repos(), &apply_params())
285	        .unwrap_err()
286	        .to_string();
287	    assert!(
288	        err.contains("not on main"),
289	        "expected off-main refusal, got: {err}"
290	    );
291	}
292	
293	/// Scenario 2c: `@` has trailing writes and local+remote both
294	/// modify the same file differently. Rebase produces conflicts;
295	/// sync fails and op-restores both repos. The trailing content
296	/// must survive the revert — jj documents that op restore rolls
297	/// back operation history without clobbering the working copy.
298	#[test]
299	fn sync_conflict_preserves_trailing_at_on_revert() {
300	    let fx = Fixture::new("trailing-conflict");
301	    let remote_claude = fx.base.join("remote-claude.git");
302	    push_from_clone(
303	        &fx.base,
304	        &remote_claude,
305	        "claude2",
306	        "shared.txt",
307	        "remote-version\n",
308	        "feat: remote shared",
309	    );
310	    // Local commit on main with conflicting content
311	    add_local_commit(
312	        &fx.claude,
313	        "shared.txt",
314	        "local-version\n",
315	        "feat: local shared (conflicting)",
316	    );
317	    // Trailing writes on new @
318	    fs::write(fx.claude.join("trailing.jsonl"), "{\"line\":3}\n").expect("write trailing file");
319	
320	    let pre_main = cid(&fx.claude, "main");
321	    let pre_remote = cid(&fx.claude, "main@origin");
322	
323	    let err = sync_repos(&fx.repos(), &apply_params())
324	        .unwrap_err()
325	        .to_string();
326	    assert!(
327	        err.contains("conflicts"),
328	        "expected conflict error, got: {err}"
329	    );
330	
331	    // Post-revert: main and main@origin back to pre-sync.
332	    assert_eq!(cid(&fx.claude, "main"), pre_main, "main reverted");
333	    assert_eq!(
334	        cid(&fx.claude, "main@origin"),
335	        pre_remote,
336	        "main@origin reverted"
337	    );
338	    // No conflicts remain.
339	    let conflicts = jj(
340	        &fx.claude,
341	        &[
342	            "log",
343	            "-r",
344	            "conflicts()",
345	            "--no-graph",
346	            "-T",
347	            r#"commit_id ++ "\n""#,
348	        ],
349	    );
350	    assert!(
351	        conflicts.trim().is_empty(),
352	        "no conflicts should remain after revert"
353	    );
354	    // Trailing content preserved on disk.
355	    assert_eq!(
356	        fs::read_to_string(fx.claude.join("trailing.jsonl")).unwrap(),
357	        "{\"line\":3}\n",
358	        "trailing content preserved across op restore"
359	    );
360	}
361	
362	/// Scenario 3: local has commits not yet pushed; sync classifies
363	/// `ahead` and leaves the local bookmark alone even under
364	/// `--no-check`.
365	#[test]
366	fn sync_ahead_is_noop() {
367	    let fx = Fixture::new("ahead");
368	    add_local_commit(&fx.work, "local.txt", "local\n", "feat: local only");
369	    let ahead_head = cid(&fx.work, "main");

371	    assert_eq!(cid(&fx.work, "main"), ahead_head);
372	}
373	
374	/// Scenario 4: clean divergence — both sides advance main on
375	/// different files; sync rebases local onto remote and the result
376	/// is conflict-free.
377	#[test]
378	fn sync_diverged_rebases() {
379	    let fx = Fixture::new("diverged");
380	    let remote_code = fx.base.join("remote-code.git");
381	    let remote_head = push_from_clone(
382	        &fx.base,
383	        &remote_code,
384	        "work2",
385	        "remote.txt",
386	        "remote\n",
387	        "feat: remote only",
388	    );
389	    add_local_commit(&fx.work, "local.txt", "local\n", "feat: local only");
390	
391	    sync_repos(&fx.repos(), &apply_params()).expect("sync should succeed");
392	
393	    // Remote tracking bookmark now points at the pushed remote commit.
394	    assert_eq!(
395	        cid(&fx.work, "main@origin"),
396	        remote_head,
397	        "main@origin should match pushed remote commit"
398	    );
399	    // Local main is ahead of remote — rebased local commit sits on top.
400	    let main_after = cid(&fx.work, "main");
401	    assert_ne!(

403	        "local main should be ahead of remote after rebase"
404	    );
405	    // Remote is an ancestor of local post-rebase.
406	    let anc = jj(
407	        &fx.work,
408	        &[
409	            "log",
410	            "-r",
411	            &format!("{remote_head}::{main_after}"),
412	            "--no-graph",
413	            "-T",
414	            r#"commit_id.short(12) ++ "\n""#,
415	        ],
416	    );
417	    assert!(
418	        !anc.trim().is_empty(),
419	        "remote should be ancestor of local after rebase"
420	    );
421	    // No conflicts.
422	    let conflicts = jj(
423	        &fx.work,
424	        &[
425	            "log",
426	            "-r",

431	        ],
432	    );

434	}

436	/// Scenario 5: conflicting divergence — both sides modify the
437	/// same path differently. Rebase produces conflicts; sync fails
438	/// and reverts both repos to their pre-sync state.
439	#[test]
440	fn sync_diverged_conflict_reverts() {
441	    let fx = Fixture::new("conflict");

475	    );

481	            "-r",

487	    );

content.text:
The test is written and it **passes** — sync does update the main bookmark in the two-clone scenario, so I couldn't reproduce what you saw.

What I added, in `src/sync/integration_tests.rs`:

- A `clone()` helper (extracted from `push_from_clone`, which now reuses it).
- `sync_clone_ffs_main_after_peer_push` — the exact shape you described: clone B is created first, clone A then commits a file and pushes to the shared bare remote, and `sync_repos` (apply mode, `--no-check`) runs on clone B. It asserts B's `main` fast-forwards to A's pushed head, `@` is empty, and `@-` sits on the new main. All assertions hold.

`cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, and the full suite (383 + CLI tests) are green. I have not committed anything — the change is sitting in `@` for you to fold into a cycle.

Since the mechanism works in the test, your real-world case likely differs in some detail. Things worth checking about that run:

- **Was it `--check` (the default)?** Check mode never fast-forwards via `act_on_state` — though `jj git fetch` itself auto-ffs a *tracked* bookmark, so even check mode usually moves `main`. If your local `main` had moved (ahead/diverged), fetch won't touch it.
- **Was the repo classified `Ahead` or `Diverged`?** An `Ahead` local main is deliberately left alone; `Diverged` rebases only under `--no-check`.
- **Which repos did scope resolve to?** If the sync ran against a different `-R`/scope than the repo you inspected, main there would be untouched.

If you can share the exact command you ran plus `jj log -r 'main | main@origin | @'` from that repo, I can pin down which path it took — or turn that state into a failing test.

message.content: Is that test you created is using `vc-x1 sync` it doesn't look like it?

content.text: You're right — it calls `sync_repos()` in-process (the library function behind the subcommand) with apply-mode params, which is equivalent to `vc-x1 sync --no-check -R <clone-b>` but skips the actual CLI layer: arg parsing, scope/repo resolution, and the installed binary. Let me look at how the existing `tests/cli_*.rs` tests drive the real binary and add a true CLI-level version.

content.content: mod.rs

content.content:
vc-x1 0.66.0

Clone a dual-repo project

Usage: vc-x1 clone [OPTIONS] <TARGET> [NAME]

Arguments:
  <TARGET>
          Source to clone — URL, owner/name shorthand, or local path.
          
          - URL: `git@host:owner/name(.git)?`, `https://...(.git)?`
          - owner/name shorthand: resolves to
            `git@github.com:owner/name.git`
          - Local path: `./X`, `../X`, `/X`, `~/X`, `~`, `.`, `..`
            (passed directly to `git clone`)

  [NAME]
          Destination dir name in cwd [default: derived from TARGET]

Options:
      --por
          Create a plain single repo (no `.claude/` companion)

  -V, --version
          Print the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as the first line, then continue. With
          no subcommand, prints the banner and exits.
          
          Replaces clap's auto-version (which would exit after printing): the
          banner now rides along with normal subcommand execution rather than
          gating it, so scripts can capture the version *and* the command's
          output in one invocation.

      --dry-run
          Dry run — show what would be done without executing

  -v, --verbose...
          Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace

      --log <LOG>
          Log file path (captures all levels)

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')
---
vc-x1 0.66.0

Create a new dual-repo project

Usage: vc-x1 init [OPTIONS] <TARGET> [NAME]

Arguments:
  <TARGET>
          Target — URL, owner/name shorthand, path, or bare NAME.
          
          - URL: `git@host:owner/name(.git)?`, `https://...(.git)?`
            — used as-is; config not consulted.
          - owner/name shorthand: resolves to
            `git@github.com:owner/name.git`; config not consulted.
          - Path: `./X`, `../X`, `/X`, `~/X`, `~`, `.`, `..` — is the
            directory path; remote resolved via `--repo` chain.
          - Bare NAME: becomes NAME.git; remote resolved via
            `--repo` chain.

  [NAME]
          Repo directory name override (URL / owner/name forms only).
          
          - URL / owner/name forms: repo created at `cwd/<NAME>`
            instead of the URL-derived name.
          - Path / bare-NAME forms: error if given (TARGET already
            names the repo).

Options:
      --account <NAME>
          Account name — picks `[account.<a>]` from user config.
          
          - Without this flag, `[default].account` (or top-level
            `[repo]` shorthand) is used.
          - Meaningful only with Path or bare-NAME targets — URL /
            owner/name targets supply the remote directly.

  -V, --version
          Print the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as the first line, then continue. With
          no subcommand, prints the banner and exits.
          
---
vc-x1 0.66.0

Dual-repo commit+push+finalize in one resumable command.

Collapses today's manual Commit-Push-Finalize Flow into a
single subcommand with two interactive approval gates and a
state machine with persistent progress so interruptions can
resume without re-doing completed stages.

Stages: preflight (fmt/clippy/test) → review (approve diff)
→ message ($EDITOR / --title+--body, approve text) →
commit-app → commit-claude (skipped if clean) → bookmark-both
→ push-app → finalize-claude. Failures in commit-app /
commit-claude / bookmark-both roll both repos back via
`jj op restore` to the snapshot recorded before commit-app.
After push-app succeeds the remote boundary is crossed and
recovery is forward-only.

Non-interactive use: pass both --title and --body plus --yes
to skip the review gate. Saved state carries title/body
across resumes so only the first invocation needs them.

Usage: vc-x1 push [OPTIONS] [BOOKMARK]

Arguments:
  [BOOKMARK]
          Bookmark to advance in both repos (positional form of `--bookmark`).
          
          Accepting a positional lets the common case read as `vc-x1 push main`
          without the `--bookmark` ceremony; `--bookmark` is kept as an alias
          for scripts and for composition with other args. The two forms
          conflict if both supplied.

Options:
      --bookmark <BOOKMARK>
          Bookmark to advance in both repos (flag form; see positional)

  -V, --version
          Print the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as the first line, then continue. With
          no subcommand, prints the banner and exits.

      --restart
          Clear any saved state file and start from stage 1

  -v, --verbose...
          Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace

      --from <STAGE>
          Explicit stage to jump to (advanced / debug use)

          Possible values:
          - preflight:       Run fmt / clippy / test / install / retest
          - review:          Present diff for the first approval gate
          - message:         Compose / edit the commit message; present for
            second gate
          - commit-app:      Commit the app repo
          - commit-claude:   Commit the `.claude` session repo (skipped if
            empty)
          - bookmark-both:   Advance both bookmarks to `@-`
          - push-app:        `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`
          - finalize-claude: `vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --squash --push <b>
            ...`

      --log <LOG>
          Log file path (captures all levels)

      --step
          Pause between every stage for an interactive approval gate

      --status
          Print where the saved state thinks we are and exit

      --recheck
          Re-run `preflight` even on resume (default: skip if last run
          succeeded)

      --no-finalize
          Stop before `finalize-claude` so it can be run manually

      --dry-run
          Print the exact commands for every stage without side effects

      --title <STR>
          Commit title (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage)

      --body <STR>
          Commit body (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage)

  -y, --yes
          Auto-approve interactive prompts (review gate, message-edit
          confirmation). Required when `--title` / `--body` aren't both supplied
          in non-interactive contexts

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')
===
          
          Replaces clap's auto-version (which would exit after printing): the
          banner now rides along with normal subcommand execution rather than
          gating it, so scripts can capture the version *and* the command's
          output in one invocation.

      --repo <CAT[=VAL]>
          Repo target — `<cat>` or `<cat>=<val>`.
          
          - `<cat>` looks up the value via the account chain
            in the user config.
          - `<cat>=<val>` uses the literal value, no config
            lookup needed.
          - Specific category meanings (`remote`, `local`,
            …) depend on the consumer subcommand.

  -v, --verbose...
          Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace

      --log <LOG>
          Log file path (captures all levels)

      --por
          Create a plain single repo (no `.claude/` companion)

      --dry-run
          Dry run — show what would be done without executing

      --private
          Create private GitHub repos (default: public).
          
          - Only meaningful when the resolved provisioner is
            `gh repo create` (GitHub URL or `--repo remote` whose
            value points at GitHub).

      --push-retries <PUSH_RETRIES>
          Max push retries after repo creation [default: 5]
          
          [default: 5]

      --push-retry-delay <PUSH_RETRY_DELAY>
          Seconds between push retries [default: 3]
          
          [default: 3]

      --use-template <CODE[,BOT]>
          Seed repos from template directories.
          
          Value is `CODE[,BOT]`. Default bot path is `<CODE>.claude`
          (file-name concat, not path join — templates are siblings).
          
          - With `--scope=por`: only `CODE` is used; passing `,BOT`
            is fatal (no session side to seed).
          - Non-hidden contents copied recursively; hidden entries
            (names starting with `.`) are skipped — init writes its
            own hidden files.
          - If a copied tree has a `README.md`, its first line is
            rewritten to `# <repo-name>`.

      --config <none|PATH>
          Override the canned `.vc-config.toml` write.
          
          - Absent: write the canned `.vc-config.toml`.
          - `--config none`: skip writing entirely.
          - `--config <path>`: copy `<path>` to `.vc-config.toml`
            (bytewise; no schema validation).

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

attachment.snippet:
1	//! CLI subprocess test for `vc-x1 sync` — the "two machines" flow.
2	//!
3	//! Drives the whole scenario through the binary: `vc-x1 init` a
4	//! project with local bare remotes, `vc-x1 clone` it twice (trA /
5	//! trB), change + `vc-x1 push` on trA, then `vc-x1 sync` on trB and
6	//! verify trB's `main` moved to the pushed head. Raw `jj` is used
7	//! only to inspect results, never to mutate.
8	//!
9	//! Two init/clone incompatibilities are worked around inline (both
10	//! candidates for real fixes, tracked in notes):
11	//!
12	//! - init's local bare remotes leave HEAD at `refs/heads/master`
13	//!   while the pushed branch is `main`, so a later `jj git clone`
14	//!   has no default branch to auto-track and `vc-x1 clone` errors
15	//!   → point HEAD at `main` with `git symbolic-ref`.
16	//! - init names the session remote `remote-claude.git`, but clone
17	//!   derives `<code-source-stem>.claude.git` → symlink
18	//!   `remote-code.claude.git` → `remote-claude.git`.
19	
20	mod common;
21	
22	use std::fs;
23	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
24	use std::process::Command;
25	
26	use common::{CliFixture, run_ok};
27	
28	/// Directory holding the `vc-x1` binary under test, for prepending
29	/// to `PATH` so subcommands that re-invoke `vc-x1` (push preflight,
30	/// finalize) find the same binary.
31	fn bin_dir() -> PathBuf {
32	    Path::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_vc-x1"))
33	        .parent()
34	        .expect("binary has a parent dir")
35	        .to_path_buf()
36	}
37	
38	/// `PATH` value with the test binary's dir prepended.
39	fn test_path() -> String {
40	    let orig = std::env::var("PATH").unwrap_or_default();
41	    format!("{}:{orig}", bin_dir().display())
42	}
43	
44	/// Run `jj <args>` in `dir` (inspection only) with `HOME` pointed
45	/// at the fixture's isolated home; assert success and return
46	/// trimmed stdout.
47	fn jj(home: &Path, dir: &Path, args: &[&str]) -> String {
48	    let out = Command::new("jj")
49	        .args(args)
50	        .env("HOME", home)
51	        .current_dir(dir)
52	        .output()
53	        .expect("spawn jj");
54	    assert!(
55	        out.status.success(),
56	        "jj {args:?} failed in {}: {}",
57	        dir.display(),
58	        String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr)
59	    );
60	    String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stdout).trim().to_string()
61	}
62	
63	/// Resolve `rev` to its short commit id in `repo`.
64	fn cid(home: &Path, repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
65	    jj(
66	        home,
67	        repo,
68	        &["log", "-r", rev, "--no-graph", "-T", "commit_id.short(12)"],
69	    )
70	}
71	
72	/// Write a minimal jj user config under the fixture's isolated HOME
73	/// so jj invocations spawned inside `vc-x1` have an identity.
74	fn write_jj_config(home: &Path) {
75	    let dir = home.join(".config/jj");
76	    fs::create_dir_all(&dir).expect("mkdir jj config dir");
77	    fs::write(
78	        dir.join("config.toml"),
79	        "[user]\nname = \"cli-test\"\nemail = \"cli-test@example.com\"\n",
80	    )
81	    .expect("write jj config");
82	}
83	
84	/// Point a bare remote's HEAD at `main` (init leaves it at
85	/// `refs/heads/master` — see module docs).
86	fn set_head_main(bare: &Path) {
87	    let out = Command::new("git")
88	        .args(["symbolic-ref", "HEAD", "refs/heads/main"])
89	        .current_dir(bare)
90	        .output()
91	        .expect("spawn git symbolic-ref");
92	    assert!(
93	        out.status.success(),
94	        "git symbolic-ref failed in {}: {}",
95	        bare.display(),
96	        String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr)
97	    );
98	}
99	
100	/// Two `vc-x1 clone`s of one `vc-x1 init`ed project: trA pushes a
101	/// change with `vc-x1 push`; `vc-x1 sync` on trB must move trB's
102	/// `main` to the pushed head and reposition `@` onto it.
103	#[test]
104	fn cli_sync_moves_main_after_peer_push() {
105	    let fx = CliFixture::new("sync-peer-push");
106	    write_jj_config(&fx.home);
107	
108	    // vc-x1 init ./tr --repo local=<base>
109	    run_ok(
110	        fx.cmd()
111	            .current_dir(&fx.base)
112	            .args(["init", "./tr"])
113	            .arg("--repo")
114	            .arg(format!("local={}", fx.base.display())),
115	    );
116	
117	    // Workarounds — see module docs.
118	    set_head_main(&fx.path("remote-code.git"));
119	    set_head_main(&fx.path("remote-claude.git"));
120	    std::os::unix::fs::symlink(
121	        fx.path("remote-claude.git"),
122	        fx.path("remote-code.claude.git"),
123	    )
124	    .expect("symlink session remote");
125	
126	    // vc-x1 clone <code-remote> trB / trA (B first, so its
127	    // main@origin is the pre-push head). Absolute source path —
128	    // the derived session source is resolved from the clone's
129	    // target dir, so a relative path would not resolve.
130	    let code_remote = fx.path("remote-code.git");
131	    for name in ["trB", "trA"] {
132	        run_ok(
133	            fx.cmd()
134	                .current_dir(&fx.base)
135	                .arg("clone")
136	                .arg(&code_remote)
137	                .arg(name),
138	        );
139	    }
140	    let tr_a = fx.path("trA");
141	    let tr_b = fx.path("trB");
142	    let pre_main = cid(&fx.home, &tr_b, "main");
143	
144	    // Change + vc-x1 push on trA. `--from review` skips the cargo
145	    // preflight (the fixture is not a Rust project); `--no-finalize`
146	    // stops before finalize-claude (out of scope here, and its
147	    // detached form logs to a fixed /tmp path); PATH carries the
148	    // binary under test for push's internal `vc-x1` calls.
149	    fs::write(tr_a.join("from-a.txt"), "from clone A\n").expect("write trA change");
150	    run_ok(fx.cmd().current_dir(&tr_a).env("PATH", test_path()).args([
151	        "push",
152	        "main",
153	        "--from",
154	        "review",
155	        "--title",
156	        "feat: from clone A",
157	        "--body",
158	        "add from-a.txt",
159	        "--yes",
160	        "--no-finalize",
161	    ]));
162	    let pushed = cid(&fx.home, &tr_a, "main");
163	    assert_ne!(pre_main, pushed, "trA's push should advance the remote");
164	
165	    // vc-x1 sync --no-check on trB.
166	    run_ok(
167	        fx.cmd()
168	            .current_dir(&tr_b)
169	            .env("PATH", test_path())
170	            .args(["sync", "--no-check"]),
171	    );
172	
173	    assert_eq!(
174	        cid(&fx.home, &tr_b, "main"),
175	        pushed,
176	        "trB's main should move to trA's pushed head"
177	    );
178	    assert_eq!(
179	        cid(&fx.home, &tr_b, "@-"),
180	        pushed,
181	        "trB's @ should be repositioned onto the new main"
182	    );
183	}
